atoms.semantic_claim: The top-level `reth` crate exposes an `async-context` feature that enables its EVM feature and propagates async-context support into `reth-evm` and `reth-evm-ethereum`.

destinations.evidence: The destination manifest contains the exact feature line at the same crate surface; `evm = ["dep:reth-evm", "dep:reth-evm-ethereum", "dep:reth-revm"]` remains the feature fanout target immediately below it.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact manifest-line preservation: the destination top-level `reth` crate exposes the same feature name and enables the same three feature targets as the downstream patch.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run a package feature resolution or `cargo check -p reth --features async-context` and assert removal of either `reth-evm/async-context` or `reth-evm-ethereum/async-context` fails to expose async-context behavior through the top-level crate.
atoms.notes: Sibling repos were searched for supporting async-context feature surfaces; the source atom itself is a `reth` crate manifest facade and maps exactly in the v2.2 Reth port.

summary: The downstream `reth` Cargo manifest change adds a single async-context feature facade, and that exact feature line is present in the v2.2 Reth port destination manifest.
notes: Queue line 99 was verified as `M	crates/ethereum/reth/Cargo.toml`; only this file's diff hunk was analyzed. No destination implementation edits or accounting metadata were changed.
